query: Klarna Germany capital efficiency platform €900 million facility €5 billion financing
b_issues: One result contains obvious irrelevant contamination/mixed content (the InvestingNews snippet includes unrelated Rio Tinto/BHP/AmeriTrust text). Another snippet states the facility size is "$900mn" instead of €900 million, introducing a currency error. Some B snippets are thinner/truncated and omit key mechanics present in A.
reasoning: Content A more clearly and reliably contains the facts needed to answer the query: Klarna added Germany to its capital efficiency platform via its first forward flow and warehouse financing agreement in Germany; the facility is €900 million; it is a two-year agreement; Klarna sold a portfolio of German Fair Financing term loans and will continue selling newly originated receivables on a rolling basis; and Klarna expects this to support up to €5 billion of German Fair Financing term-loan origination over the remaining life of the program. These facts appear consistently across multiple reliable/redundant sources, including Klarna investor relations and Yahoo reposting the Business Wire release. Content B also has the core answer, but it is weakened by the contaminated unrelated snippet and the incorrect "$900mn" currency reference, which makes it less dependable for an LLM answering from the search results alone.
